clinical_note.history_of_present_illness: Patient presents with weight gain over the past seven months, attributed to Tryptanol for depression. Reports tiredness but denies appetite changes or endocrine symptoms.
clinical_note.review_of_systems: Positive for weight gain and tiredness. Negative for appetite changes, bloating, nausea, gastrointestinal discomfort, increased thirst, urination, or temperature intolerance.
clinical_note.physical_exam: BMI 32. No swelling in legs or feet. Thyroid gland normal. Blood pressure and heart rate healthy.

assessment.summary: Weight gain likely a side effect of Tryptanol.

clinical_note.plan: Refer to psychiatry to review antidepressant options. Log weight weekly. Continue current routines. Follow up after psychiatry consult. Continue Tryptanol for now. Monitor for sudden weight increase, swelling, breathing issues, or mood changes.
